lede: A look at August 30 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Félix Sánchez (hurdler) and Johanna Braddy, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2020–2021 U.S. troop withdrawal from Afghanistan
events.desc: The last remaining American troops leave Afghanistan, ending U.S. involvement in the war.

events.title: Mohammad-Ali Rajai
events.desc: President Mohammad-Ali Rajai and Prime Minister Mohammad-Javad Bahonar of Iran are assassinated in a bombing. The office of Iran's Prosecutor General blames the People's Mujahedin of Iran.

events.title: 1974 Mitsubishi Heavy Industries bombing
events.desc: A powerful bomb explodes at the Mitsubishi Heavy Industries headquarters in Marunouchi, Tokyo. Eight are killed, 378 are injured. Eight left-wing activists are arrested on May 19, 1975, by Japanese authorities.

events.title: Thurgood Marshall
events.desc: Thurgood Marshall is confirmed as the first African American Justice of the Supreme Court of the United States.

events.title: Vietnam
events.desc: Vietnamese prison guards led by Trịnh Văn Cấn mutiny at the Thái Nguyên penitentiary against local French authority.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Germans defeat the Russians in the Battle of Tannenberg.

events.title: James VI and I
events.desc: King James VI of Scotland holds a masque at the baptism of Prince Henry at Stirling Castle.

events.title: Guru Ram Das
events.desc: Guru Ram Das becomes the Fourth Sikh Guru/Master.

events.title: Pope Paul II
events.desc: Paul II succeeds Pius II as pope of the Catholic Church.

events.title: AD 70
events.desc: Titus ends the siege of Jerusalem after destroying Herod's Temple.

births.name: Alexander Lukashenko
births.line: Belarusian marshal and politician, 1st President of Belarus

births.name: Ernest Rutherford
births.line: New Zealand-English physicist and chemist, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Naguib Mahfouz, Egyptian journalist and author, Nobel Prize laureate
Mohammad-Ali Rajai, Iranian politician, 2nd President of Iran

observances.text: Christian feast day: Alexander of Constantinople (Eastern Orthodoxy)
Christian feast day: Blessed Alfredo Ildefonso Schuster
Christian feast day: Blessed Eustáquio van Lieshout
Christian feast day: Blessed Stephen Nehmé (Maronite Church / Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Charles Chapman Grafton (Episcopal Church)
